threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You order a pint at the pub and ask for a ‘lager’, only to be met with a raised eyebrow. It’s all about the ales, mate.
Caught a glimpse of a lad in a full dinosaur costume on the N35 last night, doing a chicken dance. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I spotted the old lady feeding pigeons at the playground while a group of kids practiced breakdancing nearby. That's Neukölln in a nutshell.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a kebab shop next to a gallery, where a local artist sells paintings while the scent of döner wafts through the air.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone's local when they can run from a barking dog on the tracks to catch the 14 without missing a beat. Pure instinct.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading a sea of Muni riders at Civic Center today; only in SF will you find a bus stop turned into a concert hall.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think ‘tapas’ is a meal, but locals know it’s just a snack before something hearty, like a proper ‘plat’ at noon.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their wide-eyed confusion at the metro map—it’s not a puzzle, just a labyrinth.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the bus stop is called 'the bus stop' instead of 'the pub by the corner shop.'
Last night on the 35, a guy sang the entire 'Bohemian Rhapsody' to a shocked couple. They didn't even applaud when he finished.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy in a tracksuit arguing passionately with a pigeon outside Reuterplatz, I knew I was home. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a bar where they serve cocktails in old cassette tapes. It’s a blast from the past with a twist!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they get annoyed at tourists staring at their phones, oblivious to the BART shutdown announcements.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: The Muni bus I was on stopped suddenly today because a man tried to skateboard through the double doors. Classic Tenderloin chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the best time to visit Plaça del Sol is at night. But the real magic happens in the morning when the locals grab coffee.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they hesitate at intersections, unsure if they should wait for the light or just cross—locals know the chaos is part of the charm.
